reasoning: The market probability was ~93% on Feb 19, 2026, indicating strong skepticism about a ban materializing by the end of March. The UK government and Ofcom are clearly scrutinizing X following the Grok deepfakes scandal in January 2026. However, blocking a social media platform under the Online Safety Act requires Ofcom to establish non-compliance following a formal investigation and then apply for a court order—a standard that Ofcom itself describes as a "high bar." As of mid-to-late February 2026, Ofcom is still operating in a "research phase" and conducting behavioral audits of X and Grok. Due to the lengthy administrative and legal processes required to enact business disruption measures (a nationwide ISP block), and X taking some mitigations (e.g., paywalling Grok image generation), a functional block implemented by UK ISPs like BT before the March 31, 2026 deadline is highly implausible. The prospect of Musk voluntarily geoblocking the UK on such a short timeline also seems minimal. Therefore, X.com is exceedingly likely to remain accessible in the UK on March 31.
